title: Typography
intro:
Texto is the single text primitive — every piece of UI text in a Gravitate screen goes through it. One component, three axes: category sets the scale step and rendered element, appearance sets the color role, weight overrides the default 500. Never reach for raw

/ tags or antd Typography directly.

blocks.body:
Texto wraps antd Typography: `h1`–`h5` render semantic `<h*>` elements via antd `Title`, `p1`/`p2`/`label` render antd `Paragraph` (a block-level div), and `heading`/`heading-small` render antd `Text` (an inline span) with built-in uppercase and 5px letterspacing. Pick the category for the role, not the size you want — the scale is em-based, so every step inherits and multiplies the container's font size.

Three axes cover everything: `category` (scale step), `appearance` (color role), `weight` (font weight, defaults to 500). Anything beyond that — alignment, transforms — has a dedicated prop (`align`, `textTransform`). If you find yourself passing `style` for type styling, you are working around the component instead of with it.

blocks.title: Categories
blocks.caption: Nine of the ten categories at default appearance and weight: h1 (2.5em) down to h5 (1.25em), body p1 and the slightly larger p2, and the uppercase letterspaced heading / heading-small eyebrows. Not shown: label (0.9em fine print).

blocks.title: Category scale
blocks.intro: Exactly ten valid categories. p3 and h6 do not exist — they silently fall through to an unstyled text branch.

rows.name: h1 — 2.5em
rows.when: Hero numbers and page-level display text. Rare; one per screen at most.

rows.name: h2 — 2em
rows.when: Large stat callouts and dashboard KPIs.

rows.name: h5 — 1.25em
rows.when: Section titles inside cards and panels; pairs with appearance="medium" weight="600" textTransform="uppercase" for section headers — h5 is not uppercase on its own.

rows.name: p1 — 1em
rows.when: Default body text. The category you get when you pass none.

rows.name: p2 — 1.1em
rows.when: Emphasized body text — p2 is LARGER than p1, not fine print. Also the standard category for field labels and helper text with appearance="medium".

rows.name: label — 0.9em
rows.when: The smallest step: captions, table-adjacent metadata, true fine print.

rows.name: heading — 1.2em
rows.when: Uppercase letterspaced eyebrow above a content block. Renders an inline span, always bold and uppercase — no extra styling needed.

rows.name: heading-small — 1em
rows.when: Compact eyebrow for dense panels and drawer section breaks.

blocks.title: Appearances
blocks.caption: Seven appearances on p1: primary (brand green), secondary (the theme's INFO accent — teal here, never gray), medium and light (identical subdued gray), error, success, warning.

blocks.title: Appearance → color mapping
blocks.intro: Appearance names are color roles resolved through antd theme tokens. Hex values sampled from the default light theme.

rows.use: Body copy. The default — omit the prop.
THE gray for subdued text: field labels, helper text, section headers.
Alias of medium in the current package — prefer medium for consistency.
Placeholder-grade text. Also resolves to the medium gray today.
Brand-emphasis text tied to the primary action color.
Info-accent text ONLY. This is never gray — it is blue/teal depending on theme.
Positive states: published, passing, in range.
Caution states: stale data, soft thresholds.
Text on dark or color-filled surfaces.

blocks.title: Weights
blocks.caption: Weights 400–700 on p1. Omitting weight renders 500 — pass weight="400" explicitly when you need true regular.

blocks.title: Texto props
blocks.intro: Verified against @gravitate-js/excalibrr 5.2.1 (the version installed in the demo). Unrecognized extra props spread onto the root element.

rows.notes: The only ten valid values. p3 and h6 are not in the type union and silently break at runtime.
Color role. Use medium for gray; secondary is the blue/teal info accent.
Internally `weight || 500` — omitting the prop renders 500, never the browser-default 400.
Right-align numerics in stat layouts with align="right".
First-class prop — never pass textTransform through style. heading/heading-small are already uppercase.
Escape hatches. style merges last and wins — keep it for spacing only, never for type styling.

blocks.title: Common patterns
blocks.caption: The four workhorse recipes: uppercase section header (h5 + medium + 600 + explicit uppercase transform), field label (p2 + medium, uppercase), bold field value (p1 + 600), and helper text (p2 + medium).

blocks.title: Canonical usage — a labeled stat block
blocks.code:
import { Texto, Vertical } from '@gravitate-js/excalibrr'

<Vertical gap={4}>
  <Texto category="heading-small" appearance="medium">
    Rack Margin
  </Texto>
  <Texto category="h2" weight="600">
    $0.0425/gal
  </Texto>
  <Texto category="p2" appearance="medium">
    Trailing 7-day average vs. OPIS low
  </Texto>
</Vertical>
blocks.note: Eyebrow in heading-small (uppercase comes free), value in h2, helper in p2 + medium. Money is decimal dollars — $0.0425/gal, never cents symbols.

items.do: Use appearance="medium" for gray, subdued text.
items.why: secondary maps to the theme's colorInfoText — a blue/teal accent. medium is the antd secondary gray.

items.do: Stay inside the ten categories: p1, p2, label, heading, heading-small, h1–h5.
items.why: Invalid categories fall through to a plain antd Text branch with an undefined font size — no error, just wrong text.

items.do: Pass weight="400" when a spec calls for regular weight.
items.why: Texto renders `weight || 500` — unweighted text is always medium-weight.

items.do: Use the textTransform and align props.
items.why: The props exist for exactly this; style is reserved for spacing escape hatches.

items.do: Set all UI text with Texto.
items.dont:
Drop raw

/

tags or import antd Typography directly.
items.why: Raw tags skip the scale, the 500 default weight, and the appearance color roles — screens drift off-system one tag at a time.

items.title: appearance="secondary" is BLUE, not gray
items.detail: secondary resolves to antd colorInfoText — the info accent (teal in the default theme, blue in others). Every "why is my label blue" bug traces here. Gray text is appearance="medium".

items.title: p3 and h6 silently break
items.detail: Valid categories are only p1, p2, label, heading, heading-small, h1–h5. Anything else misses every render branch and falls to antd Text with fontSize: undefined — it renders, looks almost right, and is wrong.

items.title: p2 is larger than p1
items.detail: p2 is 1.1em, p1 is 1em. p2 is the emphasized body step, not fine print. The smallest category is label (0.9em).

items.title: medium, light, and hint are currently the same gray
items.detail: All three map to antd type="secondary" (rgba(0,0,0,0.45) in light theme). Standardize on medium so intent stays legible in code.

items.title: Default weight is 500, not 400
items.detail: Texto applies `fontWeight: weight || 500`. Designs that assume regular-weight body text need an explicit weight="400".

items.title: The scale is em-based and inherits container font size
items.detail: h1 is 2.5em of whatever the parent sets, not a fixed pixel size. Inside a container that shrinks its font-size (dense grids, compact drawers), every Texto shrinks with it.

items.title: Categories change the rendered element
items.detail: h1–h5 render real heading tags, p1/p2/label render a block-level div (antd Paragraph), heading/heading-small render an inline span. Inline vs. block matters in flex rows — eyebrows sit inline; body steps take the full line.
